atomicity.message: Staged payload is one Phase 2 unit: land-06 staged delete/rename payload and feature-detection support, limited to core.go staged-entry parser/hash behavior, core_test.go coverage, and TestPreCommitHandlesStagedDeletion in commit_test.go. Verifier replies from BrightOwl and ScarletMarsh agree the shared judgment cells pass and no unit-7 VerifierPolicy or other out-of-scope hunks are staged.

conditions.message: The staged Go code and tests satisfy the available lint/style checks: gofmt-clean staged blobs, no diff-check errors, and relevant tests pass.
evidence: ScarletMarsh read backpressure/lint-rules.md and inspected the staged payload scope.
git diff --cached --name-status showed exactly cmd/burpvalve/commit_test.go, internal/backpressure/core.go, and internal/backpressure/core_test.go.
git diff --cached --check -- cmd/burpvalve/commit_test.go internal/backpressure/core.go internal/backpressure/core_test.go produced no output.
Each staged blob matched gofmt output.
go test ./internal/backpressure -run 'Staged|Payload|Feature|Rename|Delete' passed; go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Staged|Delete|Rename|Commit' passed; go test ./... passed.

conditions.message: The staged implementation centralizes staged file status parsing and payload metadata in shared helpers instead of duplicating delete/rename handling paths.
evidence: BrightOwl inspected git diff --cached --name-status/stat and found only the three land-06 payload files staged, with 374 insertions and 33 deletions.
StagedFiles delegates to StagedEntries/stagedEntryPaths; GitStagedReader and GitCommittedReader both use parseGitNameStatus; HashStagedPayload uses stagedPayloadEntries and sortStagedEntries.
Shared helper symbols parseGitNameStatus, normalizeGitStatus, sortStagedEntries, stagedPayloadEntries, HashStagedPayload, and StagedPayloadFile are staged once.
Focused backpressure and cmd tests passed, and go test ./... passed.

BrightOwl verified staged tests cover deleted paths not being read, rename old_path preservation, shell-sensitive paths, delete metadata affecting the hash, and real git staged/committed rename/delete parsing.

ScarletMarsh independently read anti-reward-hacking.md, inspected the same staged implementation/tests, and reported no shortcut that fakes evidence, bypasses the gate, or claims unsupported completion.

conditions.message: The changed parser, payload-hash, feature-detection, and pre-commit deletion behavior has direct unit and CLI regression coverage.
evidence: BrightOwl found TestHashStagedPayloadHandlesDeletedRenamedAndShellSensitivePaths, TestGitStagedReaderHandlesRealRenameAndSpecialPaths, TestGitCommittedReaderHandlesDeleteAndRename, TestPreCommitHandlesStagedDeletion, and TestDetectFeaturesExplicitFlag in the staged diff.
The hash test covers delete, rename old_path, shell-sensitive paths, deterministic included paths, and hash change when delete metadata is removed.
The real-git tests cover staged rename/special paths and committed delete/rename parsing.
The commit test covers staged deletion reaching attestation-written and exposing deleted status.
Focused package tests passed.

conditions.message: The product code parses and records staged deletion metadata but does not perform destructive operations; actual file removals in the staged payload are test-fixture removals inside temporary repositories.
evidence: ScarletMarsh read backpressure/destructive-operations.md and inspected the staged payload.
git diff --cached --name-status showed no staged file delete or rename in this commit; the three staged paths are modified source/test files.

Search for destructive terms found os.Remove only in tests: TestPreCommitHandlesStagedDeletion removes src/app.go in a fixture repository and fixtureCommittedDeleteAndRename removes src/delete.go in t.TempDir().
The staged product code changes git diff/diff-tree readers and hash metadata; it does not add rm/reset/force-push/deploy/production mutation behavior.

conditions.message: The staged data-integrity surface was inspected: delete and rename metadata enter the payload hash/plan, deleted files are not read from the index, real git parsing is covered, and deterministic sorting is centralized.
evidence: HashStagedPayload writes path/status/old_path/git_status into the hash, skips StagedFileContent for deleted entries, includes IncludedFiles, and uses sortStagedEntries by path/old_path/status.
parseGitNameStatus handles R/C old path plus new path and normalizeGitStatus maps A/C/D/M/R/T to stable statuses.
Tests assert deleted path was not read, delete metadata affects hash, rename old_path is preserved, the same staged rename payload hashes stably, and the committed reader handles delete/rename.
Focused backpressure tests and go test ./... passed.

conditions.message: The staged security-relevant command/path surface was inspected. Git calls use fixed argv rather than shell strings, shell-sensitive paths are covered, and no secrets/auth/permission expansion is staged.
evidence: BrightOwl and ScarletMarsh both treated changed git subprocess/path handling as the governed surface and returned pass.

GitStagedReader uses exec.CommandContext(ctx, 'git', 'diff', '--cached', '--name-status', '-M', '-z'); GitCommittedReader uses exec.CommandContext(ctx, 'git', 'diff-tree', '--no-commit-id', '--name-status', '-M', '-r', '-z', base, 'HEAD').
Tests create and stage paths containing spaces, leading dashes, tabs, and newlines with git add --; tests assert those paths are preserved in payload metadata.
Searches of the staged diff found no credentials, auth logic, tenant logic, PII handling, or external API calls.
go test ./... passed.

conditions.message: The staged payload does not introduce a declared performance budget or benchmark-governed surface; it keeps the existing one-git-command payload reader shape and adds deterministic sorting over staged entries.
evidence: ScarletMarsh read backpressure/performance-budget.md and inspected the staged payload.
core.go changes existing git diff/diff-tree calls from name-only to name-status and adds deterministic sorting of staged entries.
The staged diff does not add concurrency, network calls, large-tree scans beyond existing staged diff readers, or explicit performance budgets.
Search of staged diff for latency/memory/benchmark/goroutine/time.Sleep/context.WithTimeout found no performance-budget surface.
go test ./... passed; ScarletMarsh did not treat that as a benchmark, only regression evidence.

conditions.message: The staged commit-gate/autonomy surface was inspected. The new deletion path still requires response-backed attestation flow and stops at attestation-written, while explicit feature detection avoids implicit bead-evidence coupling.
evidence: TestPreCommitHandlesStagedDeletion runs burpvalve commit with --feature and --responses, expects the attestation-written stop, and asserts StatusAttestationWritten rather than final pass.
DetectFeatures explicit feature now sets DiffCluster 'explicit:<feature>' and leaves SourceBead empty, avoiding implicit bead evidence from --feature alone.
TestDetectFeaturesExplicitFlag now asserts SourceBead == '' and DiffCluster == 'explicit:burpvalve-prs-09a'.
--no-verify appears only on baseline commits inside temporary test repositories, not as product guidance or a path around this repo's gate.
BrightOwl read backpressure/autonomy-boundary.md and inspected the staged autonomy surface.
